headline: Scotland end a 28-year World Cup wait — and start by going top of the group
summary: Scotland reached their first World Cup since 1998 (a 28-year absence), having sealed qualification dramatically with two stoppage-time goals to pip Denmark. In their opener on June 13, 2026 they beat Haiti 1-0 through a John McGinn strike (28') at Gillette Stadium — and, because Brazil and Morocco drew 1-1 earlier the same day, the Tartan Army sit TOP of Group C above Brazil after matchday 1.
angles: 28 years of hurt ended — and they win their opener
Scotland sit ABOVE Brazil in the group after one game (Brazil drew Morocco)
John McGinn the unlikely hero with the only goal
Can Scotland actually escape a group with Brazil and Morocco? (prediction bait)
Dramatic qualification: two stoppage-time goals to pip Denmark, now rewarded
